checks.a_analysis: The HTML output successfully replicates the visual organization of the source PDF. The hierarchical structure of price lists for different vendors (Broadcom, Sophos, Trellix, CrowdStrike, Cisco) is maintained using consistent headings and table layouts. Information flow follows the original document's intent.

All tables from the source PDF, including complex pricing grids with multiple year columns (e.g., CrowdStrike on PDF page 16 and Presidio on PDF page 19), are correctly rendered as HTML tables. Row and column alignments are preserved, headers are distinct, and the data remains readable and logically organized within the tabular structures.

The source document is primarily text-based, consisting of pricing tables and descriptions. There are no meaningful images, charts, or diagrams in the source PDF that are missing from the HTML output. Branding elements appear to be text-based in the source and are represented correctly.

A thorough visual scan of the rendered HTML confirms that no body content (paragraphs or tables) has been duplicated. While section headings appear in the navigation area and the main body, this is an intentional accessibility feature and not a content defect.
